takeaways: Chiron Real Estate's $425M senior housing entry closes today — REITs continue dominating off-market M&A as Healthpeak's Janus Living signs $400M in LOIs and Invesco grabs a majority stake in a $2.04B Class A portfolio.
Welltower posted 22.1% SHOP NOI growth in Q1 and raised full-year FFO guidance to $6.21–$6.35; Ventas raised its 2026 senior housing investment target to $3B after closing $1.7B YTD.

AI-driven layoffs accelerate: Meta (8,000), Intuit (3,000), Cloudflare (1,100), and Coinbase (700) all announced cuts in May as 100K+ tech jobs have been eliminated YTD in a structural restructuring around AI.

items.headline: Chiron Real Estate closes $425M senior housing entry, deal completes today
items.summary: Chiron Real Estate is closing its $249M acquisition of two Alexandria, VA luxury communities (163-unit Landing and 129-unit Riviera) from Silverstone Senior Living today, with a $176M Pinnacle North Bethesda deal to follow in October. Marks Chiron's first senior housing investment two months after rebranding — signals deep institutional appetite for trophy assets that Lloyd Jones competitors are now chasing.

items.headline: Welltower Q1 SHOP NOI grows 22.1%, raises 2026 FFO guidance
items.summary: Welltower reported $728.7M net income, $1.47 normalized FFO (+23% YoY), revenue up 38% to $3.35B, and 16.4% same-store NOI growth in Q1 2026. Raised full-year FFO guidance to $6.21–$6.35 per share. The SHOP flywheel — record demand against constrained supply — is delivering alpha that operators like Lloyd Jones can capture in this cycle.

items.headline: Ventas raises 2026 senior housing investment target to $3B after $1.7B YTD
items.summary: Ventas grew total company NOI 14% and SHOP same-store cash NOI more than 15% YoY in Q1, with 310 bps of occupancy gains and 5% revenue-per-occupied-room growth. Raised 2026 investment guidance from $2.5B to $3B, all senior-housing focused. Confirms thesis that institutional capital is rotating heavily into senior housing.

items.headline: Omega Healthcare CEO Pickett to retire Oct 1, Gourmand promoted
items.summary: Omega Healthcare Investors (NYSE: OHI) announced CEO Taylor Pickett will step down on October 1 with President Matthew Gourmand ascending to CEO. Planned transition gives operating partners a runway to renegotiate triple-net terms — watch for shifts in OHI's coverage requirements and master lease structure heading into 2027.

items.headline: Intuit cuts 3,000 jobs (17%) to refocus on AI products
items.summary: Intuit announced it is laying off about 3,000 employees — roughly 17% of staff — and redeploying resources into AI-native product development. Part of a broader May 2026 wave that has put 100K+ tech jobs out YTD as enterprises restructure org charts around agentic AI rather than software.

items.headline: Fed holds rates at 3.5-3.75%, J.P. Morgan sees no cuts in 2026
items.summary: The Fed paused again in April, keeping the federal funds rate at 3.5-3.75% set at its March meeting. J.P. Morgan Global Research now expects no further cuts in 2026, with the next move potentially a 25bp hike in Q3 2027. CRE borrowing costs hover near 6.57% vs. average cap rates of 6.34% — a near-zero spread that pressures underwriting math on new senior housing acquisitions.

items.headline: $1.5 trillion in CRE loans maturing through end of 2026
items.summary: Approximately $1.5T in commercial real estate loans will mature by year-end 2026, with elevated rates and selective lending creating distress particularly in office and increasingly in multifamily. Refinancing risk creates a pipeline of forced-seller opportunities — well-capitalized senior housing buyers should be positioned to act on motivated sellers in H2.

items.headline: ServiceNow unveils Project Arc desktop AI agent at Knowledge 2026
items.summary: ServiceNow used its Knowledge 2026 conference to launch Project Arc, a desktop AI agent that runs inside NVIDIA's OpenShell sandbox and executes multi-step enterprise work without pre-built workflows, governed by ServiceNow's AI Control Tower. Signals the shift from chatbot AI to agents that actually do back-office work — including the lease admin, AR, and reporting tasks senior housing operators currently staff.

items.headline: Senior housing occupancy to exceed 90% in 2026, NIC says
items.summary: NIC MAP projects average senior housing occupancy will exceed 90% in 2026, potentially hitting the highest level in the 20-year history of the data. Inventory growth fell to its lowest level since 2006, and units under construction in Q1 totaled just 0.4% of inventory with construction cycles stretched to 29 months. Supply-demand dynamics are the most favorable for operators in two decades.

items.headline: Operators expand IL portfolios as occupancy crosses 91%
items.summary: Independent living occupancy passed 91% in Q1 2026 — the first time in a decade across both primary and secondary markets — prompting operators including the largest national platforms to add IL units. The 'Woodstock Generation' (oldest boomers turning 80 this year) is driving the demand, with preferences shifting toward higher-touch hospitality and wellness programming.

items.headline: CBRE projects CRE investment volume to climb 16% to $562B in 2026
items.summary: CBRE expects U.S. CRE investment volume to rise 16% to ~$562B in 2026, near the pre-pandemic annual average. Colliers separately forecasts a 15-20% jump as institutional and cross-border capital reenters the market. Senior housing is the highest-conviction property type for that incremental capital, intensifying competition for quality stabilized deals.

items.headline: NHI narrows SHOP focus after $560M NHC portfolio sale
items.summary: National Health Investors (NHI) is repositioning its portfolio after a $560M sale of its National HealthCare Corporation properties, narrowing its SHOP focus to higher-growth markets. Capital recycling among healthcare REITs is creating off-market deal flow as REITs prune skilled nursing exposure and lean into senior housing.

items.headline: Meta cuts 8,000 jobs (10%) starting May 20 in AI restructure
items.summary: Meta announced ~8,000 layoffs starting May 20 — about 10% of its 77,986 Q1 headcount — alongside parallel announcements from Cloudflare (1,100 / 20%) and Coinbase (~700 / 14%). Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong framed it as a structural shift to smaller, AI-augmented teams, signaling the layoffs are permanent role eliminations rather than cyclical reductions.

items.headline: JPM: 73% of business leaders expect revenue growth; 49% cite economic uncertainty as top concern
items.summary: J.P. Morgan's 2026 Business Leaders Outlook finds 73% expecting revenue growth and 64% expecting higher profits, but 49% rank economic uncertainty as their top challenge, followed by revenue/sales growth (33%) and tariffs (31%). Leaders are optimistic on their own company performance but cautious on the macro — a familiar setup for value-driven M&A.

items.headline: Proptech investment hits $16.7B in 2025, $1.7B deployed in January 2026 alone
items.summary: Global proptech investment reached $16.7B in 2025, up 67.9% YoY, with $1.7B deployed in January 2026 alone — a 176% YoY jump. AI-first property management platforms (Grotto AI, MARC, Boom) are absorbing the largest share. Operators should expect more vendor consolidation and faster feature roadmaps over the next 12 months.

items.headline: AI in Real Estate Summit hits NYC June 4 during NY Tech Week
items.summary: 300 real estate and technology leaders gather in NYC on June 4 for the AI in Real Estate Summit during NY Tech Week, hosted by the US Proptech Council. Expect agenda focused on agentic AI deployment, vendor due diligence, and the move from pilots to production — a useful pulse-check on where peer operators are placing tech bets.

items.headline: GLP-1s named top health trend of 2026 with FDA expansion and price cuts
items.summary: GLP-1 medications topped U.S. News's 2026 health trends list (52% of expert panel), now FDA-approved for heart disease, kidney protection, and sleep apnea with clinical trials showing 20% reduction in major heart events independent of weight loss. Negotiated pricing brings Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, and Zepbound to $245/month. Implications for senior housing: healthier, longer-lived prospects with shifting care-need profiles.

items.headline: Agentic AI emerges as next proptech phase
items.summary: ICSC reports agentic AI is the next major proptech phase, moving past content generation into autonomous procurement, pricing, inventory, and event programming. Retailers and real estate operators are already deploying agents for back-office work — early movers in senior housing are using them for lease admin, reporting, and vendor management.

items.headline: Wellhub: 89% of employees perform better when prioritizing wellbeing
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life Wellness report finds 89% of employees say they perform better when prioritizing wellbeing, framing wellness investment as one of the highest-ROI talent strategies. Relevant for senior housing operators competing for caregivers and ops staff in tight labor markets — wellness benefits are showing measurable engagement and retention gains.

items.headline: Emotional fitness emerges as new wellness category for 2026
items.summary: 2026 wellness trends introduce 'emotional fitness' as a distinct category covering cognitive performance and emotional resilience, alongside biomarker-based nutrition, AI wellness coaches, and at-home biomarker testing. Senior living programming and amenity design can lean into these themes to differentiate against legacy operators.
